steps.title: Set up the percent increase formula
steps.content:
If the dragonfly count increased by 18.75% from May 1 to June 11, then the June 11 count equals the May 1 count multiplied by $(1 + 0.1875)$.
$$133 = x \times 1.1875$$
where $x$ is the number of dragonflies on May 1.

steps.title: Backsolve the answer choices
steps.content:
Backsolving — testing choice C (108):
$$108 \times 1.1875 = 128.25 \neq 133$$
Fails. Testing choice D (112):
$$112 \times 1.1875 = 133$$
This works! That confirms D is correct.
